briefing.opening: Hello everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for June 22, 2026.

sections.title: 🌱 Domestic Efforts for Agricultural and Ecosystem Protection
sections.content: Today, active discussions and collaborations took place domestically to address the vulnerability of agricultural production and ecosystems due to climate change. The Korea Agro-Fisheries & Food Trade Corporation (aT) and the National Institute of Agricultural Sciences signed a cooperative agreement to respond to climate change and agricultural supply instability, exploring specific measures such as advancing long-term storage technology for cabbage [1, 3, 17, 18, 19, 20, 25, 28, 34]. Furthermore, Yeoju City is strengthening technology-based adaptation efforts, such as introducing a smart irrigation system that controls water for potato fields via smartphones, to prevent drought damage [7, 39, 50]. However, the decline of pine trees [4, 38, 42, 53] and the decrease in bee populations [10, 21, 37, 48] due to climate change were still highlighted as serious issues. In particular, research indicating that temperate organisms are more significantly affected than tropical organisms [6, 14] underscores the urgency of biodiversity conservation. The Korea Forest Service held an expert forum to discuss innovative pine tree management and the establishment of science-based policies amidst this climate crisis, seeking ways to maintain healthy forest ecosystems [44, 51].

sections.title: 📰 International Discussions on Nuclear Safety and Climate Change Impacts
sections.content: International attention is focused on the impact of climate change on the safety of nuclear power facilities. The Korea Atomic Energy Research Institute (KAERI), in collaboration with the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA), hosted a research cooperation meeting in Seoul until the 26th, under the theme of 'Climate Change and Nuclear Facility Safety' [5, 13, 15, 16, 45, 46]. At this meeting, experts from around the world explored the impacts of climate change on nuclear power plants and sought solutions to potential threats, particularly the fear of 'flooded nuclear power plants' due to the climate crisis [22, 27, 41]. This is regarded as having established an important forum for discussions on energy security and stable power supply in the era of climate change.

sections.title: 🌱 Carbon Neutrality Practices and Strengthening Corporate ESG Management
sections.content: Domestic companies are strengthening ESG management to achieve carbon neutrality goals, and local governments are also exploring various practical measures. Hansol Holdings analyzed climate change risks and presented a carbon neutrality roadmap through its sustainability report [8, 11, 43, 54, 89, 99], while Hankook & Company Group is operating the '2050 Carbon Neutrality One Team,' consolidating energy capabilities among its affiliates [64, 67, 74, 77, 82, 84, 87, 93, 95, 103, 108, 112, 113, 115, 118, 120, 123, 128, 138, 140, 225, 244]. Yuseong-gu, Daejeon, achieved a reduction of 2,128 tons of greenhouse gases through its carbon neutrality points system [66, 80, 109, 135, 144], and Gwangmyeong City received the 'Minister's Award from the Ministry of Climate, Energy, and Environment' for its green building policies, demonstrating its competitiveness as a leading carbon-neutral city [62, 65, 97, 110, 122, 139]. Furthermore, efforts to encourage citizen participation and train specialized personnel are continuously underway, including Eunpyeong-gu's carbon neutrality stamp tour [57, 58, 59, 133, 136, 137, 141, 143] and Gangbuk-gu's training program for environmental volunteer specialists [90, 129].

sections.title: 🌱 Investment and Policies for Renewable Energy Expansion
sections.content: Large-scale investments by domestic companies and policy support initiatives for renewable energy expansion are prominent. GS E&C has partnered with global investors to launch a 3 trillion won renewable energy development project, setting a goal to secure 1.5 GW of power by 2035 [145, 151, 152, 157, 163, 167, 169, 176, 180, 182, 183, 188, 189, 193, 195, 200, 201, 207, 215, 218, 219, 222]. This is evaluated as a strategy to accelerate its transformation from a construction company to an energy developer [199]. Meanwhile, Representative Heo Jong-sik has proposed an amendment to the Meteorological Act to legislate 'renewable energy weather information,' aiming to enhance the accuracy of renewable energy generation forecasts and strengthen the policy foundation [146, 154, 155, 164, 170, 171, 174, 205, 210, 214, 217, 223]. However, the recent sharp decline in renewable energy-related stocks [191] could lead to a cooling of investor sentiment, suggesting the need for careful market management.

sections.title: 🌍⚠️ Severity of Global Climate Change and International Cooperation
sections.content: Globally, the severe impacts of climate change are spreading, and international efforts to address them are continuing. In Europe, record-breaking heatwaves are analyzed as a consequence of climate change [236, 239], and the impacts on daily life are growing, including France's ban on alcohol sales at outdoor festivals [49] and school closures and female labor force withdrawal in India [246]. Climate change is acting as a wide-ranging threat, intensifying the El Niño phenomenon [227], severely impacting wildlife in unexpected regions [233], turning Türkiye into a major disease corridor [231], and exacerbating the child malnutrition crisis in Afghanistan [234]. Amidst these circumstances, the importance of international cooperation is further highlighted, with Norway signing a large-scale aid agreement to help Indonesia respond to climate change [238] and Lagos seeking to strengthen global partnerships [241].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing demonstrates that diverse efforts are underway both domestically and internationally to address climate change, but it also suggests that we must confront the reality of the intensifying global climate crisis. This concludes the climate briefing for June 22, 2026.

articles.title: How India’s heatwaves are shutting schools – and pushing women out of the workforce

Inside Sakshi Katyal’s city apartment, the air conditioner is blasting but it does little to relieve the stress of balancing housework and helping her five-year-old log in on a laptop to online classes. Her daughter’s school closed in May and Katyal is not clear when it will reopen. Probably not till th

summary.phenomenon: In India, schools are closing due to heatwaves exceeding 41C (105.8F), leading to women being pushed out of the workforce. For instance, one mother is experiencing stress balancing housework and online classes after her daughter's school closed in May with no clear reopening date.
summary.cause: The climate crisis is causing heatwaves, which in turn lead to schools transitioning to online classes or closing for weeks or months at a time.
summary.outlook: Working mothers are bearing the brunt of the climate crisis, facing school closures and shifts to online learning, which increases their burden of housework and childcare, often forcing them out of their jobs.
